creator: schneecrash+freebsd@gmail.com
text:
hi,

> Can you point me to newer realeses of the BIND-DLZ patch?
>
>
> http://www.freebsd.org/cgi/query-pr.cgi?pr=115956

well, i've been on a little 'odyssey' of DLZ-learnin'!

it turns out that dlz code is now 'integrated' into bind 94x release.

so, afaict, there *are* no more patches ...

that said, the integrated code has some issues, as well.  both in
general, and with building on fbsd.

so, 1st question:  should DLZ w/ bind >= v94x on freebsd still be
addressed in *this* separate port?  or in the bind94 port itself?

i dunno ...

that said, while i'm slogging away at getting it to work on freebsd,
here's my cobbled-up patch which seems to be working (for me.  note
that it's NOT currently 'generically applicable' ...)

vi bind9+dlz+bdb46_support.patch
==========================================
diff -ur bind94ORIG/Makefile bind94/Makefile
--- bind94ORIG/Makefile	Sat Sep  1 10:07:33 2007
+++ bind94/Makefile	Sat Sep  1 10:09:54 2007
@@ -42,6 +42,18 @@

 .include <bsd.port.pre.mk>

+.if defined(WITH_DLZ_DBD)
+CONFIGURE_ARGS+=    --with-dlz-bdb=yes
+.endif
+
+.if defined(WITH_DLZ_FILESYSTEM)
+CONFIGURE_ARGS+=    --with-dlz-filesystem=yes
+.endif
+
+.if defined(WITH_DLZ_STUB)
+CONFIGURE_ARGS+=    --with-dlz-stub=yes
+.endif
+
 .if defined(WITH_OPENSSL_PORT)
 CONFIGURE_ARGS+=	--with-openssl=${LOCALBASE}
 .else
Only in bind94: Makefile.orig
diff -ur bind94ORIG/work/bind-9.4.1-P1/contrib/dlz/bin/dlzbdb/Makefile.in
bind94/work/bind-9.4.1-P1/contrib/dlz/bin/dlzbdb/Makefile.in
--- bind94ORIG/work/bind-9.4.1-P1/contrib/dlz/bin/dlzbdb/Makefile.in	Sat
Sep  1 10:07:31 2007
+++ bind94/work/bind-9.4.1-P1/contrib/dlz/bin/dlzbdb/Makefile.in	Sat
Sep  1 10:13:37 2007
@@ -23,16 +23,16 @@

 @BIND9_MAKE_INCLUDES@

-DLZINCLUDES =	@DLZ_BDB_INC@
+DLZINCLUDES =	@DLZ_DRIVER_INCLUDES@

 CINCLUDES =	-I${srcdir}/include -I${srcdir}/unix/include \
                 ${ISC_INCLUDES} ${DLZINCLUDES}

-CDEFINES =      @USE_DLZ_BDB@
+CDEFINES =	    @USE_DLZ@
 CWARNINGS =

-DLZLIBS = 	@DLZ_BDB_LIBS@
-ISCLIBS =	../../lib/isc/libisc.@A@
+DLZLIBS = 	@DLZ_DRIVER_LIBS@
+ISCLIBS =	../../../../lib/isc/libisc.@A@

 DEPLIBS =	${ISCDEPLIBS}

diff -ur bind94ORIG/work/bind-9.4.1-P1/contrib/dlz/config.dlz.in
bind94/work/bind-9.4.1-P1/contrib/dlz/config.dlz.in
--- bind94ORIG/work/bind-9.4.1-P1/contrib/dlz/config.dlz.in	Sat Sep  1
10:07:31 2007
+++ bind94/work/bind-9.4.1-P1/contrib/dlz/config.dlz.in	Sat Sep  1 10:09:54 2007
@@ -208,7 +208,7 @@
 			# Check other locations for includes.
 			# Order is important (sigh).

-			bdb_incdirs="/ /db42/ /db41/ /db4/ /db/"
+			bdb_incdirs="/db46/"
 			for d in $bdb_incdirs
 			do
 				if test -f "$dd/include${d}db.h"
@@ -229,10 +229,10 @@
 			# Look for libname other than libdb.so.
 			# Order is important (sigh).

-			bdb_libnames="db42 db-4.2 db41 db-4.1 db"
+			bdb_libnames="db-4.6"
 			for d in $bdb_libnames
 			do
-				if test -f "$dd/lib/lib${d}.so"
+				if test -f "$dd/lib/db46/lib${d}.so"
 				then
 					if test "$dd" != "/usr"
 					then
==========================================

so, to summarize (from a discussion i'm having on bind9-dlz-testers list ...)

"atm, i've a freebsd portfile successfully building/installing
bind-941p1, configured "--with-dlz-bdb --with-dlz-stub", and linked
against port-installed Berkely DB 4.6.19. and, "dlzbdb" builds as
well."

of course, to 'genericize' this, the portfile should include ALL the
--with-dlz-* options, and be made to use USE_BDB from fbsd's
make.conf.

hth a little!  interested in further comments ...

cheers!
